name: Expiratory time
description: Expiratory time (Te) — duration of the expiratory phase of a ventilator breath, in seconds.

en.name: Expiratory time
en.shortDescription: Expiratory time (Te) — duration of the expiratory phase of a ventilator breath, in seconds.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Expiratory time (Te) is the duration of the expiratory phase of a ventilator breath — the time during which the patient exhales. Together with inspiratory time it sets the inspiratory:expiratory (I:E) ratio. In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, set or measured by the ventilator. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. It is reported in seconds (s).

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED and LOINC concepts for this duration. All are reported in seconds.

- *4353622 / 250820008 — Expiratory time (SNOMED)*

**Note on units**: reported in **seconds** (`8555 / s — second`, UCUM), the recommended unit.

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when not otherwise qualified: *4353622 / 250820008 — Expiratory time*.
- This is one specific breath-phase duration; inspiratory time, expiratory time and inspiratory pause time are separate concept sets (separate variables).

fr.shortDescription: Temps expiratoire (Te) — durée de la phase expiratoire d’un cycle ventilatoire, en secondes.
